description: Golden identifier set the automated pipeline should retrieve/resolve for the depression screening / triage decision-support reference case (Case 7). Companion to the HRV/DR/warfarin/sepsis/AFib/melanoma goldens. This is a DEVICE / Software-as-a-Medical-Device case (intervention_type=device) and is regulatory-NULL for the system under evaluation: there is NO FDA product code, 510(k), De Novo grant, or PMA for a depression SCREENING/DETECTION algorithm. The scored FDA codes are the adjacent-but-different landscape - depression-named TREATMENT stimulators (JXK cranial electrotherapy, MUZ implanted autonomic nerve, both class 3), a depression THERAPY software code (SAP, class 2, therapeutic not screening; clearances K231209 Rejoyn), a behavioral-health PILOT placeholder with no regulation number (SIE), and the De Novo PRECEDENT PATHWAY for a novel psychiatric assessment/diagnosis aid demonstrated by two other-indication grants: DEN200069 Cognoa ASD Diagnosis Aid -> created code QPF (autism; subsequent 510(k) K243558 Canvas Dx), and DEN110019 NEBA System -> created code NCG (neuropsychiatric EEG aid, ADHD). No same-purpose depression-screening predicate exists, which pushes THIS device toward De Novo. Use in the ablation harness: score recall = golden ids present in the bundle's records (FDA codes / K / DEN / NCT EXACT; literature as a >=K resolvable-ID floor per COMPARISON.md Lesson 2), precision = on-topic rate. Every id in 'required' resolved live 2026-07-09 (PMIDs via E-utils esummary, DOIs via Crossref works, product codes via openFDA device/classification, K & DEN via openFDA device/510k, NCTs via ClinicalTrials.gov v2).
reference_case: Depression screening / triage aid: an algorithm estimating the likelihood of a depressive disorder in adults from patient-reported questionnaire responses (e.g. digital PHQ-9) and/or passively collected behavioral/speech signals, deployed as a screen/flag-for-assessment aid (does NOT diagnose). Reference standard = validated instrument (PHQ-9) plus a clinician structured/semistructured diagnostic interview. Non-standalone decision support; the pivotal endpoint is the screen-positive flag's diagnostic accuracy against the reference standard, not an isolated model AUC.
mesh_note: Condition authored condition-forward ('depression / depressive disorder screening'), so Case-4 Finding A cannot bite. 'Depression' maps to MeSH 'Depression' (D003863) and 'Depressive Disorder' (D003866); whether the '+hierarchy' axis diverges here is determined empirically by the sweep and written up in ablation_findings.md - not asserted in advance. Note the literature here is a curated methodology + reference-instrument set (STARD/TRIPOD+AI/DECIDE-AI/QUADAS-2/Riley + PHQ-9 validation & IPD meta-analysis + Obermeyer fairness precedent), the same targeting-ceiling profile as HRV/melanoma - a generic 'depression screening' query is not expected to rank these into the top-15.

match_policy.required_min_coverage: All ids in 'required' should be present for full recall. 'field_grounding' is the per-field breakdown following each source's role in the spec's SOURCE INVENTORY. Like DR/warfarin/sepsis/AFib/melanoma, depression has a scored 'ncts' category, but ONLY the 2 load-bearing enrollment-magnitude anchors (NCT07220343 n=25 pilot; NCT06792175 n=500) that Field 5 uses as concrete small-vs-large-cohort magnitude context. The three CT.gov keyword COUNTS the spec cites (76 / 118 / 33 studies) are landscape context, not identifiers, and are not scored. Depression has NO 'fda_pma_numbers' (no PMA in this space) and NO 'fda_nda_numbers' (device/SaMD, not a drug or biologic).
match_policy.resolution_pass: PMID -> E-utils esummary; DOI -> Crossref works; product_code -> openFDA device/classification; K (510k) -> openFDA device/510k k_number; DEN (De Novo) -> openFDA device/510k (De Novo grants indexed with a DEN k_number - verified live: DEN200069 returns Cognoa ASD Diagnosis Aid, DEN110019 returns NEBA System); NCT -> ClinicalTrials.gov v2. A required id that fails to resolve is a TEST FAILURE, not a silent drop.
match_policy.regulatory_null_for_screening: NULL: an openFDA device/classification + device/510k sweep this session returned NO product code, 510(k), De Novo, or PMA for a depression SCREENING/DETECTION algorithm. The scored codes are the adjacent landscape: JXK/MUZ = depression TREATMENT stimulators (class 3); SAP = depression THERAPY software (class 2, reg 882.5801; clearance K231209 Rejoyn is a digital therapeutic, not a screen); SIE = a behavioral-health PILOT placeholder (class N, no regulation number, not an authorization). QPF (reg 882.1491, from DEN200069 Cognoa ASD; 510(k) K243558 Canvas Dx) and NCG (reg 882.1440, from DEN110019 NEBA) are the De Novo PRECEDENT pathway for novel psychiatric assessment/diagnosis aids in OTHER indications (autism, ADHD-EEG) - they demonstrate the pathway a depression-screening SaMD would follow, but neither authorizes depression screening. This makes De Novo (not 510(k) to an existing predicate) the anchored U.S. pathway and makes signal-validation (Field 2) + a justified operating point (Field 3) pivotal, since no established numeric accuracy benchmark exists.

expected_conclusions.regulatory_class: Software as a Medical Device diagnostic/assessment aid; the anchored U.S. pathway is De Novo classification (novel device type, class II expected) as demonstrated by the Cognoa ASD (DEN200069 -> QPF) and NEBA (DEN110019 -> NCG) psychiatric-aid precedents - NOT a 510(k) against an existing depression-screening predicate.
expected_conclusions.nearest_depression_code_is_therapy_not_s: SAP (Computerized Behavioral Therapy Device for Depressive Disorders) is therapeutic; depression-named cleared devices JXK/MUZ are treatment stimulators.

regulatory_null_evidence.claim: No FDA product code, 510(k) clearance, De Novo grant, or PMA exists for a depression SCREENING/DETECTION algorithm. The only depression-specific software code (SAP) is for THERAPY, and depression-named cleared devices (JXK, MUZ) are treatment stimulators.
regulatory_null_evidence.how_verified: openFDA device/classification and device/510k searched this session for depression-screening/detection software: returned only treatment devices (JXK, MUZ), a therapy software code (SAP; clearance K231209 Rejoyn), and a behavioral-health pilot placeholder (SIE, class N, no regulation number). The De Novo precedent for a novel psychiatric assessment/diagnosis aid comes from OTHER indications - DEN200069 Cognoa ASD -> QPF (autism; 510(k) K243558 Canvas Dx) and DEN110019 NEBA -> NCG (neuropsychiatric EEG, ADHD) - all scored in 'required' as the adjacent-but-different-purpose landscape. openFDA has no dedicated device/de_novo endpoint (404); De Novo grants are recoverable only as DEN k_numbers within device/510k, so De Novo coverage is indirect. No same-purpose depression-screening predicate was found.

coverage_gaps_expected: Full-text of the cited methodology guidelines (STARD/TRIPOD+AI/DECIDE-AI/QUADAS-2/Riley) - only PubMed metadata + Crossref records verified, not line-by-line content
FDA De Novo decision-summary PDFs for DEN200069 / DEN110019 (openFDA summary text field not retrieved)
A definitive FDA classification determination for THIS device (a regulatory judgment obtainable only via an FDA pre-submission, not a database fact)
Drugs@FDA / drug labeling / FAERS (not applicable - software/SaMD, not a drug or biologic)
openFDA device/udi, device/registrationlisting, device/event (MAUDE), enforcement endpoints returned 404 in the generating environment - no UDI / registration / adverse-event cross-check
